channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Addresses Party Members After TMC’s West Bengal Election Loss
item.description: Following All India Trinamool Congress’s (TMC) significant defeat in the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections, party chief Mamata Banerjee addressed party members at her Kalighat residence on Saturday....

item.title: Mitch Marner Thrives Post-Leafs: Lessons for Toronto’s Team Management
item.description: Mitch Marner’s performance in the playoffs with the Vegas Golden Knights highlights the challenges he faced during his tenure with the Toronto Maple Leafs. While in Toronto, Marner often appeared over...

item.title: Thousands of Australians face loss of Dutch citizenship due to strict nationality laws
item.description: Since the late 19th century, the Netherlands has enforced strict rules that make it difficult for citizens to hold dual nationality. Thousands of Dutch-born Australians have lost their citizenship aut...

item.title: University of the Philippines study tracks 1,368 marine mammal strandings from 2005 to 2022
item.description: A collaborative study by the University of the Philippines (UP) and the University of Canberra documented 1,368 marine mammal stranding events across the Philippines over an 18-year period, from 2005 ...

item.title: Tambuwal Explains Why Peter Obi and Kwankwaso Left ADC
item.description: Former Sokoto State Governor, Senator Aminu Tambuwal, don talk say the exit of Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso from the African Democratic Congress (ADC) no be because of any quarrel or crisis inside th...

item.title: Prime Minister Directs Tighter Import Controls and End to Tax Incentives for Locally Made Goods to Boost Tanzanian Industries
item.description: On February 12, 2026, Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba issued important directives during the launch of a new steel sheet manufacturing plant by Lodhia Group in Mkuranga, Pwani Region. He instructed the...

item.title: Bato dela Rosa Under Immigration Lookout; Senate Shooting Investigation Ongoing
item.description: On May 15, 2026, significant developments unfolded in Philippine politics and public safety. Senator Bato dela Rosa is now under an immigration lookout order, raising questions about his movements and...

item.title: US-China Security Tensions During Trump-Xi Beijing Summit
item.description: During President Trump’s visit to Beijing, what seemed like warm camaraderie between him and Xi Jinping for cameras was shadowed by intense friction between US and Chinese security officials. The summ...

item.title: Experts Debunk Spencer Pratt's Claims About 'Super Meth' Amid LA Mayoral Race
item.description: Spencer Pratt, former reality TV star and current Los Angeles mayoral candidate, has been using the term 'super meth' throughout his campaign to portray a heightened drug crisis affecting the city’s h...

item.title: Parliament questions use of Sh300 million set aside for wildlife compensation
item.description: Members of Parliament have raised concern over the use of Sh300 million that was meant to support compensation for victims of human-wildlife conflict. The money is reported to have been spent on consu...

item.title: EFCC clarifies UUTH visit was administrative, denies making arrests or assaulting staff
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has officially denied reports claiming that their operatives arrested or assaulted a medical doctor and other staff members during an incident at th...

item.title: Climate-related flood risks are increasingly threatening UK household finances and property security
item.description: The article explains how climate change is set to increasingly affect household finances in the UK, even before physical damage from flooding becomes widespread. Using New Orleans as an extreme exampl...

item.title: Assumption College Student Council Supports Petition to Remove Sen. Loren Legarda's Portrait from Wall of Empowered Women
item.description: The student council of Assumption College San Lorenzo has thrown its support behind a petition seeking the removal of Senator Loren Legarda's portrait from the school's Wall of Empowered Women. In a s...

item.title: RCMP investigate targeted shooting after multi-vehicle collision in Airdrie, Alberta
item.description: Airdrie RCMP are investigating a targeted incident in which shots were fired following a multi-vehicle collision early Friday morning. The event occurred on Bayside Boulevard between 8 Street and Cana...

item.title: Federal Government Lowers Petrol and Diesel Prices by Rs5 Starting May 16
item.description: The federal government has announced a reduction of Rs5 per litre in petrol and high-speed diesel (HSD), effective from May 16, as per the Petroleum Division's notification. After this adjustment, pet...

item.title: Weekend Weather Forecast: Mostly Dry and Cool with Light Showers in Eastern Areas
item.description: South Africa is set for a calm and predominantly dry weekend, with cooler temperatures prevailing across most of the country. On Saturday, the interior regions, including Gauteng, the Free State, Nort...

item.title: Tammy Tyrrell officially joins the Labor Party after previously criticising it
item.description: Tammy Tyrrell, previously an independent Senator known for her critical remarks about the Labor Party, has officially joined the party, marking a significant shift in her political stance. Over the pa...

item.title: David Beckham reaches billionaire status as first UK sportsperson
item.description: David Beckham, former England football captain and global sports icon, has officially become the first British sportsperson to reach billionaire status. His earnings over the years have come not just ...

item.title: 2009 Court Records Show Trump Listed in Epstein-Related Lawsuit
item.description: Newly discovered court documents from 2009 reveal that a woman who survived abuse by Jeffrey Epstein accused former President Donald Trump of having knowledge of Epstein's financial dealings and sexua...

item.title: Colorado Governor Grants Clemency to Tina Peters, Reducing Sentence from 9 to 4.5 Years
item.description: Colorado Governor Jared Polis granted clemency to Tina Peters, the former Mesa County Clerk, reducing her prison sentence from nine years to four and a half years. Peters was convicted for tampering w...
